perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the term confidential information (“Confidential Information”) shall mean all financial and other nonpublic information, together with notes, analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared or provided by the disclosing party in connection with the evaluation of the Acquisition. Confidential Information shall also include proprietary information concerning the respective businesses, operations and assets of the parties, including, without limitation, trade secrets, techniques, models, data, documentation, code, research, development, processes, procedures, business strategy, marketing timetables, pricing policies, financial information and other information of a similar nature, whether or not reduced to writing or other tangible form.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the term confidential information (“Confidential Information”) shall mean information deemed confidential by the receiving party. Confidential Information shall also include information the parties agree to be confidential.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Confidential Information' provides a comprehensive list of items considered confidential. The changed text significantly narrows the definition to 'information deemed confidential by the receiving party' and 'information the parties agree to be confidential.' This creates an in-text contradiction, as Section 1.3 references the 'Confidential Information it receives', implying a specific, pre-defined set of information, while the revised definition allows the receiving party to arbitrarily determine what constitutes Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party will use the Confidential Information it receives solely for the purpose of evaluating the Acquisition and not for any other purpose and, except to the extent permitted by paragraph 1.5 hereof, will keep such Confidential Information strictly confidential, provided, however, that Confidential Information may be disclosed to such Representatives as needed to know such information for the purpose of evaluating and negotiating the terms of the Acquisition and for no other purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the definition of 'Confidential Information' from a specific list of items to information 'deemed confidential by the receiving party' and 'information the parties agree to be confidential.' This contradicts the original agreement outlined in the 'Contradicted Text,' which states that each party will use the 'Confidential Information it receives solely for the purpose of evaluating the Acquisition,' implying a predetermined set of confidential data. The changed text allows the receiving party to arbitrarily decide what is confidential, undermining the original intent of the 'Contradicted Text' and creating an in-text contradiction as it breaks compatibility with the original definition of Confidential Information.

perturbation.original_text: Representatives 1.2 For purposes of this Agreement, the term representatives (“Representatives”) shall mean each party, their directors, officers and employees, as well as their counsel, accountants, consultants and other representatives in connection with the transactions contemplated hereby provided that such persons are bound by confidentiality agreements no less stringent than those in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Representatives 1.2 For purposes of this Agreement, the term representatives (“Representatives”) shall mean each party, their directors, officers and employees.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Representatives' includes counsel, accountants, and consultants, but only 'provided that such persons are bound by confidentiality agreements no less stringent than those in this Agreement.' By removing this clause, Representatives are now not necessarily bound by confidentiality agreements. This creates an in-text contradiction with section 1.3 where Confidential Information may be disclosed to such Representatives as needed to know such information for the purpose of evaluating and negotiating the terms of the Acquisition, because they no longer have to be tied by confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party will use the Confidential Information it receives solely for the purpose of evaluating the Acquisition and not for any other purpose and, except to the extent permitted by paragraph 1.5 hereof, will keep such Confidential Information strictly confidential, provided, however, that Confidential Information may be disclosed to such Representatives as needed to know such information for the purpose of evaluating and negotiating the terms of the Acquisition and for no other purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Representatives' as being bound by confidentiality agreements, whereas the changed text removes this requirement. The contradicted text allows disclosure of confidential information to Representatives, implying they are bound by confidentiality. By removing the confidentiality agreement requirement from the definition of 'Representatives', the changed text creates a contradiction because Representatives receiving confidential information are no longer necessarily bound by confidentiality agreements, breaking the assumption in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the parties hereto do not proceed with the Acquisition by October 31, 2007, and, in any event, within five (5) days after being so requested by either party, both parties shall return or destroy all documents furnished by the other.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the parties hereto do not proceed with the Acquisition by October 31, 2007, both parties may retain all documents furnished by the other.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement mandates that if the acquisition does not proceed by October 31, 2007, or upon request, all documents must be returned or destroyed. The change removes the obligation to return or destroy documents and allows both parties to retain them. This creates a contradiction, because the documents are still considered confidential as mentioned in Article 1, however now can be retained.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information disclosed shall be and shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. In the event that the parties hereto do not proceed with the Acquisition by October 31, 2007, both parties may retain all documents furnished by the other. Any oral Confidential Information shall remain subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the return or destruction of documents if the acquisition doesn't proceed by a specific date. The changed text permits both parties to retain these documents. This directly contradicts the principle in the Contradicted Text that confidential information remains the property of the disclosing party. By allowing retention of documents, the change weakens the protection of confidential information, which is a strong, direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Zulu and its principal shareholders agree that until October 31, 2007, or such shorter period if either party notifies the other that it no longer wishes to proceed with the Acquisition (“Term of this Agreement”), that they will not, directly or indirectly, solicit, initiate or encourage submission of proposals of offers from any third party relating to any acquisition, purchase or option to purchase an equity interest in Zulu, or any merger, consolidation or business combination with Zulu or the sale of substantially all of the assets of Zulu.
perturbation.changed_text: Zulu and its principal shareholders agree that they will not, directly or indirectly, solicit, initiate or encourage submission of proposals of offers from any third party relating to any acquisition, purchase or option to purchase an equity interest in Zulu, or any merger, consolidation or business combination with Zulu or the sale of substantially all of the assets of Zulu.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'until October 31, 2007, or such shorter period if either party notifies the other that it no longer wishes to proceed with the Acquisition (“Term of this Agreement”)' phrase, the standstill agreement is now indefinite. This creates an in-text contradiction with Article 5 which implies there is a time limit, specifically 'October 31, 2007'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties hereto will use their best efforts to enter into a definitive acquisition agreement and any other documents that may be necessary in order to consummate the Acquisition by October 31, 2007. If a definitive agreement has not been entered into by such date, then this Agreement will have no further force or effect except for Articles 1, 3, 4, 5 and 6 hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a termination date for the standstill agreement ('October 31, 2007, or such shorter period'). The changed text removes this termination date, making the standstill agreement indefinite. The contradicted text states that the entire agreement (except for specific articles) will have no further force or effect after October 31, 2007. The removal of the original time limitation directly contradicts this provision, since it now implies that the standstill could last indefinitely, while Article 5 expects it to end on October 31, 2007. This is a strong contradiction under the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' as 'essential language is removed... creating a contradiction with obligations or constraints stated in other sections.'

perturbation.original_text: The parties hereto agree that they shall not solicit business from any supplier, customer, client or contact of any other party hereto for the purpose of circumventing the relationship between such party and such supplier, customer, client and/or competing with such party during the Term of this Agreement without prior written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties hereto agree that they shall not solicit business from any supplier of any other party hereto for the purpose of circumventing the relationship between such party and such supplier without prior written consent.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference to customer, client or contact, the clause is now limited to only supplier. This creates an in-text contradiction, because 3.2 states 'Each party hereto acknowledges and agrees that the other parties hereto have a material interest in preserving the relationships they have developed with their customers and employees against impairment by competitive activities of other persons and entities'. Limiting the clause in 3.1 to just suppliers, contradicts the wider statement mentioned in 3.2 where the parties have interest in customer and employees
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party hereto acknowledges and agrees that the other parties hereto have a material interest in preserving the relationships they have developed with their customers and employees against impairment by competitive activities of other persons and entities. Accordingly, each party agrees that the restrictions and covenants contained in this Agreement are of the essence of this Agreement and constitute a material inducement by the each party to the other to disclose the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited soliciting business from suppliers, customers, clients, or contacts. The changed text narrows this prohibition to only suppliers, omitting customers, clients, and contacts. The contradicted text states that each party has a material interest in preserving relationships with customers and employees. By removing 'customer, client, contact' from the prohibition in the changed text, it directly contradicts the contradicted text, which acknowledges a material interest in preserving customer relationships from competitive activities, creating an in-text contradiction as it now allows for the solicitation of customers, clients and contacts.

perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE 5 DEFINITIVE AGREEMENTS; DISCLOSURE The parties hereto will use their best efforts to enter into a definitive acquisition agreement and any other documents that may be necessary in order to consummate the Acquisition by October 31, 2007. If a definitive agreement has not been entered into by such date, then this Agreement will have no further force or effect except for Articles 1, 3, 4, 5 and 6 hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE 5 DEFINITIVE AGREEMENTS; DISCLOSURE The parties hereto will use their best efforts to enter into a definitive acquisition agreement and any other documents that may be necessary in order to consummate the Acquisition. If a definitive agreement has not been entered into, then this Agreement will have no further force or effect except for Articles 1, 3, 4, 5 and 6 hereof.
perturbation.explanation: The original Article 5 explicitly states that the parties will attempt to enter into a definitive acquisition agreement by 'October 31, 2007' and specifies that the agreement expires on that date if no definitive agreement is reached, except for certain articles. The revised text removes the October 31, 2007 deadline entirely. Removing the date creates an in-text contradiction because it goes against the 'Term of this Agreement' mentioned in Article 2 which does have a deadline and is linked to the acquisition ending.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Zulu and its principal shareholders agree that they will not, directly or indirectly, solicit, initiate or encourage submission of proposals of offers from any third party relating to any acquisition, purchase or option to purchase an equity interest in Zulu, or any merger, consolidation or business combination with Zulu or the sale of substantially all of the assets of Zulu. In the event Zulu receives any solicitation, proposal or offer with regard to the foregoing, Zulu shall provide written notice to Swansi within two business days of its receipt by Zulu or its principals.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the 'October 31, 2007' deadline in Article 5 creates an in-text contradiction with the provided Contradicted Text. The explanation states that the removed deadline contradicts Article 2 (Term of this Agreement) which contains a deadline linked to the acquisition, making the agreement's term indefinite, thus conflicting with the purpose mentioned in the original text, even though Article 2 itself is not provided. The contradicted text concerns Zulu's obligations regarding acquisition proposals. Without a definitive end date for the acquisition agreement as stated in Article 5, Zulu's obligations under this section are now ambiguous and potentially perpetual, creating an inconsistency within the contract itself. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.
